_doc: Canonical kitp database schema. server/cmd/schema-gen reads this file and emits a CREATE-everything + INSERT-seeds SQL script. tables[] defines DDL; seed[] is always applied; demo[] is opt-in fixture data for a populated dev DB. There is no forward-only migration chain — schema changes happen by editing this file.

tables.name: user_account
tables.doc: Users. id=1 is the System User used by AUTH_MODE=off. email and oidc_sub identify OIDC subjects.

tables.name: role
tables.doc: Application role. user_role assigns these to user_account rows; role_grant lists what (card_type, process) pairs each role may invoke.

tables.name: role_mapping
tables.doc: OIDC group claim → role mapping.

tables.name: card_type
tables.doc: Built-in and user-declared card types. parent_card_type_id constrains valid parents; allow_self_parent permits tasks under tasks etc.

tables.name: attribute_def
tables.doc: An attribute definition (title, status, assignee, ...). value_type is one of text|number|bool|enum|user_ref|card_ref|card_ref[].

tables.name: attribute_def_option
tables.doc: Enumerated options for enum-typed attribute_defs (e.g. status: todo/doing/review/done).

tables.name: edge
tables.doc: An attribute_def bound to a card_type, optionally required.

tables.name: card
tables.doc: A card instance.

tables.name: activity
tables.doc: Append-only stream of card events: card_create, attr_update, tag_apply, comment, ...

tables.name: attribute_value
tables.doc: Current value of one attribute on one card. last_activity_id points at the activity row that set this value.

tables.name: process
tables.doc: Logical workflow name (e.g. card.create). process_step rows declare the ordered handler chain.

tables.name: process_step
tables.doc: One step of a process — (endpoint, action) executed in ordinal order.

tables.name: role_grant
tables.doc: (role, card_type, process) tuple authorising role-holders to invoke process on card_type.

tables.name: user_role
tables.doc: Assigns a role to a user, optionally scoped to a single card subtree.

tables.name: comment_body
tables.doc: Comment text attached to a card via an activity row of kind=comment.

tables.name: idempotency_response
tables.doc: Cache of POST responses keyed by (user, key); replayed on duplicate submission.

tables.name: user_card_sort
tables.doc: Per-user inbox ordering. Separate axis from the shared attribute_value.sort_order on the kanban.

tables.name: cas_blob
tables.doc: Content-addressed blob. address is the sha256-hex of the bytes; bytes live in cas_blob_data when storage_kind='pg'.

tables.name: cas_blob_data
tables.doc: Inline pg-storage backend for cas_blob. Absent when bytes live in an external backend (e.g. S3).

tables.name: file
tables.doc: Logical file (filename + size + mime). Bytes are stored as ordered file_chunk rows pointing at cas_blob addresses.

tables.name: file_chunk
tables.doc: Ordered CAS-address list per file. Reaper walks (file_chunk.cas_address) to keep cas_blob rows live.

tables.name: attachment
tables.doc: Card attachment. Points at a file row (and optionally a thumb file for image attachments).

indexes.name: idx_attachment_thumb_file_id
indexes.where: deleted_at IS NULL AND thumb_file_id IS NOT NULL

seed.doc: System User (id=1) + dev team. Explicit ids so role_grant / activity / attachment rows can reference them by id without lookups.
Built-in roles. 'system' holds every grant for AUTH_MODE=off; the other four are the OIDC role model.

rows.name: system
rows.doc: Internal role held by the System User in dev mode; carries every grant.

rows.name: worker
rows.doc: Can act on tasks: update status/assignee, post comments, apply tags, reorder personal inbox.

rows.name: manager
rows.doc: Worker plus the ability to create/edit/delete projects, milestones, components, and tags within scope.

rows.name: admin
rows.doc: Manager plus user role management and OIDC claim mapping.

seed.doc: System User gets the 'system' role globally.
seed.sql: INSERT INTO user_role (user_id, role_id, scope_card_id) SELECT 1, r.id, NULL FROM role r WHERE r.name = 'system' ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

seed.doc: Default OIDC group → role mappings for the dex dev stack.
Built-in attribute definitions. status is 'enum' (options seeded below).
Status enum options. Order drives the kanban column layout.
Built-in edges. ordering drives display order on the card detail panel.
Process steps. task.update_with_comment is the compositional demo: update attrs then leave a comment in one tx.
system role: every (card_type, process) tuple. Worker/manager/admin grants below are explicit per the OIDC role model.
seed.sql: INSERT INTO role_grant (role_id, card_type_id, process_id) SELECT r.id, ct.id, p.id FROM role r CROSS JOIN card_type ct CROSS JOIN process p WHERE r.name = 'system' ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

INSERT INTO role_grant (role_id, card_type_id, process_id) SELECT r.id, ct.id, p.id FROM role r CROSS JOIN card_type ct CROSS JOIN process p WHERE r.name = 'worker' AND ct.name = 'task' AND p.name IN ('card.update','comment.post','user_card_sort.set') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

seed.doc: manager grants: worker grants + create/update/delete on project/milestone/component/tag.
seed.sql: INSERT INTO role_grant (role_id, card_type_id, process_id) SELECT r.id, ct.id, p.id FROM role r CROSS JOIN card_type ct CROSS JOIN process p WHERE r.name = 'manager' AND ct.name = 'task' AND p.name IN ('card.update','comment.post','user_card_sort.set') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; INSERT INTO role_grant (role_id, card_type_id, process_id) SELECT r.id, ct.id, p.id FROM role r CROSS JOIN card_type ct CROSS JOIN process p WHERE r.name = 'manager' AND ct.name IN ('project','milestone','component','tag') AND p.name IN ('card.create','card.update','card.delete') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

seed.doc: admin grants: card.* + comment.post + user_card_sort.set across every built-in card_type.
seed.sql: INSERT INTO role_grant (role_id, card_type_id, process_id) SELECT r.id, ct.id, p.id FROM role r CROSS JOIN card_type ct CROSS JOIN process p WHERE r.name = 'admin' AND ct.name IN ('project','milestone','component','tag','task') AND p.name IN ('card.create','card.update','card.delete','comment.post','user_card_sort.set') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

demo.doc: Default Project + 3 milestones + 5 components + 8 tags + 25 dense tasks. Each card emits the same activity stream the runtime would.
